title: Apache Hadoop MapReduce Input
description:
The Apache Hadoop MapReduce library provides a set of classes for writing applications that process vast amounts of data in-memory on large clusters in a scalable manner. The MapReduce framework is designed to handle data-intensive tasks by breaking them into smaller tasks that can be executed in parallel. The input directory in the Hadoop MapReduce library is responsible for setting and adding input paths for a map-reduce job. It collaborates with the JobContext object and an array of Path objects to specify the input data for the job. The architectural role of the input directory is to provide a flexible and scalable way to specify input data for map-reduce jobs, allowing users to easily add or remove input paths without modifying the job code. The pattern used in the input directory is the Hollywood Principle, which states that each class should have only one reason to change, and that reason should be its name. This principle is followed in the input directory, where the responsibility of setting and adding input paths is well-defined and limited to the input directory.

--- DIRECTORY: lib/jobcontrol ---
TITLE: Hadoop MapReduce Architecture
DESCRIPTION: The Hadoop MapReduce architecture is a distributed processing framework that allows for the processing of large data sets across clusters of computers. It is designed to scale up from single servers to thousands of machines, each offering local computation and storage. The architecture consists of a master node and multiple slave nodes, with the master node coordinating the tasks and the slave nodes executing them. The master node also keeps track of the status of the cluster and the tasks running on it. The architecture is fault-tolerant, meaning that if a slave node fails, the master node can reassign the tasks to other slave nodes. The architecture is also scalable, meaning that it can handle large amounts of data and can be expanded by adding more slave nodes to the cluster.

TITLE: 
DESCRIPTION: Cluster: 0
Directory: lib/join

 responsibility:
- manage the join of multiple data sources
- provide a way to combine data from different sources based on a common key

collaboration:
- use a priority queue to sort the RecordReaders based on their keys
- use a JoinCollector to collect values for a given key from the child RecordReaders

architectural role/pattern:
- implement the join operation using a priority queue and a JoinCollector
- provide a way to combine data from different sources based on a common key

--- DIRECTORY: lib/map ---
TITLE: MapReduce -Mapper-
DESCRIPTION: The MapReduce -Mapper- directory is responsible for processing input data and transforming it into a format that can be easily processed by the MapReduce framework. It collaborates with other directories, such as the -Reducer- directory, to perform the final processing and generate the desired output.

The directory follows the architectural pattern of the MapReduce framework, which is a distributed processing paradigm used for processing large data sets. The MapReduce framework consists of two main components:
